REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 19, 2025 AT 9:30 AM IN LIGHT OF DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL BEING RETAINED THE DAY THE OPPOSITION WAS DUE AND IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF FILING A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING OF NO MORE THAN 3 PAGES EACH ON THE LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. GIVEN THE CIRCUMSTANCES, THE COURT WILL CONSIDER DEFENDANT'S UNTIMELY OPPOSITION. THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER WILL REMAIN IN EFFECT UNTIL THIS CONTINUED HEARING DATE. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
Off Calendar Sep-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION

Appearances: Richard Hurlburt of Legal Assistance to the Elderly (415.757.4531) for plaintiff and moving party; Eric Cha of Wright Finlay & Zak, LLPP (949.477.5050) for defendants Carrington Mortgage Service and Compu-Linc Corp.; John Steele of Steele LLP (949.222.1161) for defendant MTC Financial Inc.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Plaintiff showed a likelihood of success on the merits and irreparable harm. The Injunction is issued without Bond. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Continued To Sep-23-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION

Appearances: Cathy Mosbrucker of Legal Assistance to the Elderly (415.757.4531) for plaintiff and moving party; Eric Cha of Wright Finlay & Zak, LLP (949.477.5050) for defendants and responding parties.

Counsel inform the court they have stipulated to a 30 day continuance to continue settlement discussions. On agreement of the parties, the court continues this matter to September 23, 2025. Counsel waive notice. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
Off Calendar Aug-12-2025 Continued To Aug-19-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: CONTINUED to August 19, 2025 at 9:30 am in light of Defendant's Counsel being retained the day the opposition was due and in light of Plaintiff filing a First Amended Complaint. The parties are ordered to provide supplemental briefing of no more than 3 pages each on the likelihood of success on the merits of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Given the circumstances, the Court will consider Defendant's untimely opposition. The Temporary Restraining Order will remain in effect until this continued hearing date. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
